name: FaceSwapAI Controlled Face-Swap Input Degradation Study
purpose: A controlled test of how blur, low resolution, low light, highlight clipping and JPEG block compression change observable preflight measurements while face detection remains successful.

design.controlledVariable: Each degraded sample starts from the same public control portrait and changes one documented image property.

measurement.globalSample: Longest image side reduced to 360 pixels while preserving aspect ratio.
measurement.clipping: Percentage of sampled luminance values at or below 5, or at or above 250.
measurement.faceSharpness: Variance of a four-neighbor luminance Laplacian inside the detected face box plus 12% padding, normalized to 160x160 pixels.
measurement.jpegBlockiness: Ratio and excess of native-scale luminance differences crossing aligned 8x8 JPEG boundaries versus non-boundary neighbors inside the largest detected face box plus 25% padding.
measurement.faceDetection: FaceSwapAI production public detector; count and bounding box only, with no identity matching.
measurement.readinessRole: Each sample scored as a source image with FaceSwapAI Readiness Score v1.1.0.

limitations: This is a single-portrait controlled measurement study, not a representative sample of people, cameras or face-swap models.
The study does not create or visually grade generated face-swap outputs and therefore makes no model-accuracy or success-rate claim.
Synthetic degradations isolate variables but do not reproduce every camera, social platform or editing pipeline.
A successful face detection indicates only that a bounding box was returned; it does not prove that the image is suitable for generation.

samples.label: Gaussian blur
samples.transformation: Gaussian blur with sigma 9; dimensions retained; JPEG quality 88.
samples.observedIssue: Face-region sharpness collapses while detection still succeeds.

samples.label: Low resolution
samples.transformation: Width reduced to 180 pixels with aspect ratio preserved; JPEG quality 88.
samples.observedIssue: The detector still finds the face, but resolution and normalized sharpness fall.

samples.label: Low light
samples.transformation: Brightness multiplied by 0.32; dimensions retained; JPEG quality 88.
samples.observedIssue: Mean luminance, contrast and face-region sharpness all fall.

samples.label: Overexposed
samples.transformation: Linear RGB gain 1.8 plus offset 80; dimensions retained; JPEG quality 88.
samples.observedIssue: More than half the sampled pixels lose highlight information.

samples.label: Heavy JPEG compression
samples.transformation: JPEG quality 5 with 4:2:0 chroma subsampling; dimensions retained.
samples.observedIssue: An extreme 8x8 block-boundary ratio appears even though global contrast remains stable.
